📁 This event took place Wednesday 3rd June 2026 — registration is now closed. Archived for reference.

Executive Briefing — Archived

The AI Execution Gap is costing businesses more than they realise.

Most AI projects don't fail because of technology. They fail because the business wasn't operationally ready to run them.

The gap between adopting AI and executing with AI is widening — and that gap is where revenue leaks, competitive advantage disappears, and transformation programmes quietly stall.

This briefing is for leaders who are responsible for turning AI into measurable business results. We're not going to talk about potential. We're going to talk about execution.

This Event Has Concluded

Registration for this briefing closed on 3rd June 2026. The agenda and speaker details below are kept here for reference.

Interested in a conversation about AI execution readiness for your organisation? Reach out directly.

Full Agenda

→

Why AI projects fail commercially — the real reasons transformation programmes stall and what to do instead

→

The operational gaps AI is exposing — what broken processes, disconnected systems and manual workflows are costing you now

→

Why governance is becoming infrastructure — not a compliance checkbox, but a core part of how you operate at scale

→

From AI tools to AI operating models — what the shift actually means for your organisation's structure and leadership

→

Execution risk in the age of agentic AI — what leaders must understand before deploying autonomous AI systems

→

What businesses should audit before scaling AI — a practical framework you can apply immediately

→

The next competitive divide in enterprise AI — which businesses will pull ahead in H2 2026 and why

Your Speakers

Host
Lisa Warren
Founder & CEO, Neural Horizons AI
20+ years in data analytics, digital transformation, and AI strategy. Generated AED 9M+ in additional revenue for organisations across MENA, Europe and Asia. Named "One to Watch in 2026" by The Prime Today. Board Member, Green Economy Partnership.
Speaker
Dr Patrick McCrudden, PhD
AI Governance, Institutional Transformation & Emerging Technology Risk
Strategic leader with deep expertise in AI governance frameworks, institutional risk, and the regulatory landscape shaping how organisations must operate in an AI-first world.
Speaker
Keith Elliot
Workplace Strategy & AI-Ready Organisational Design
Specialist in building AI-ready teams, operational alignment, and the structural changes organisations need to execute with AI rather than simply adopt it.

Ready to close your AI Execution Gap?

"The companies pulling ahead in 2026 won't be the ones adopting AI fastest. They'll be the ones rebuilding their operational architecture around it." — Lisa Warren, Founder, Neural Horizons AI
